pages.raw_text:
The presence in the city of a large
number of prospective buyers for
farm lands Is taken by real estate
men as an indication that immigra-
ton will begin very early this year.


Lands ofall kinds from timber and
stone propositions down to site for
a poultry ranch are wanted, and quite
few real estate men are out in the
country. showing their best pieces of
property.


The meeting of real estate dealers
for the purpose of forming a local
organization, announced Saturday to
be held Tuesday afternoon, will be
held this evening at 1880 at the office
of Il. A. Yancey-Williams company
The promoters of the organization
are d. Z. Ham of the Palouse Land
company. +. f. Elmendorf, Phil T.
Becher r. A. Hutchinson, j. w. Nor
rison and the G. A. Yancey- Williams
company.


Among the real estate sales made
by the G.. a. Yancey- Williams COM
pany last week were 1000-acre stock
ranch, with the stock on same, near
Waterville, for $4,000; a 320-acre
wheat farm, near Waterville, for
$1,000; 100 acres timber land, near
Clayton, $500, and two lots in
Northeast addition to Ross Park,
$500.


c. L. Parks no recently acquired
four lots, through Elmendorf & Ell
mendorf, in the Fourth addition to
the Railroad addition, intends to
build four modern residences thereon.
